items.title: DoE deputy director-general charged with RM21k graft, pleads not guilty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Environment (DoE) deputy director-general Dr Norhazni Mat Sari was charged in the Sessions Court today with accepting RM21,000 in graft. The 59-year-old pleaded not guilty when the charges were read before Sessions Court judge Azura Alwi. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib informed the court that both parties had agreed on bail of RM25,000.

items.title: AG: Dashcam video, witness testimony justify murder charge in Klang DUI crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Citing clear evidence that a driver’s actions were “so imminently dangerous” that death was a near certainty, Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar today defended the decision to level a murder charge in the fatal Klang drink-driving crash. Speaking on the charge against R. Saktygaanapathy, Dusuki explained that the case meets the threshold for murder under Section 300(d) of the Penal Code, the New Straits Times reported. He noted the accused’s deliberate act of driving into the opposite lane at high speed, which created a highly perilous situation for the victim, delivery rider Amirul Hafiz Omar.

items.title: 'WFH is work, not leisure or shopping,' Chief Secretary warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Department heads must ensure staff working from home (WFH) follow rules and meet key performance indicators. Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar said the move is in line with the Public Service Reform Agenda, which emphasises productivity. Shamsul Azri said civil servants must set an example by not only carrying out instructions but also ensuring effective implementation.

items.title: Private GPs welcome revised RM10–RM80 consultation fee after 30-year freeze
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The Federation of Private Medical Practitioners’ Associations Malaysia (FPMPAM) has taken note of the gazettement of the revised general practitioner (GP) consultation fee schedule, which came into effect yesterday. Its president, Dr Shanmuganathan T.V. Ganeson, said the revision marks the first in over 30 years and should be viewed as a long-overdue correction to an outdated fee structure, rather than a conventional fee increase.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines expands China network, adds Shenzhen & Changsha
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines Bhd will launch two new destinations in China namely Shenzhen and Changsha as well as resume services to Fukuoka, Japan from Kuala Lumpur between July and September this year. Parent company Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) president and chief executive officer (CEO) Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said the new routes will be operated using its Boeing B737-8 aircraft. He added that the Fukuoka route will initially be operated using the B737-8 aircraft but the airline may deploy a larger aircraft such as the Airbus A330neo (new engine option) depending on demand and new aircraft delivery schedules.

items.title: Selangor to launch special task force Monday to combat global energy crisis
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 3 — The Selangor government will announce the establishment of a special task force by Monday at the latest, to address current challenges and their impact on the people’s lives following the global energy crisis. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said a meeting to finalise the establishment of the task force would be held today with members drawn from experts in various fields including economics, finance and industries such as oil and gas. He said programme and activity proposals would be submitted by departments and government-linked companies (GLCs), before being scrutinised for implementation by the task force.

items.title: 17 under MCMC probe for spreading false information on fuel, electricity tariffs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating 17 people suspected of spreading false information about fuel price hikes and new electricity tariffs on social media. In a statement, MCMC said as of today, statements have been recorded from eight suspects. This includes five cases related to false information about fuel price hikes and three cases concerning the new electricity tariffs.

items.title: Ramanan urges MTUC to resolve internal rifts over Gig Workers Act
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan has urged the Malaysian Trades Union Congress (MTUC) to settle its internal leadership disputes before accusing the government of failing to engage stakeholders over the Gig Workers Act. This follows a call by MTUC and several NGOs to postpone the enforcement of the Act (Act 872), citing a purported lack of meaningful consultation with workers' representatives. Ramanan said that the government remains undeterred by the union's internal politics as it advances the historic implementation of the Gig Workers Act 2025 (Act 872).

items.title: MyDigital, Oracle team up to train 300,000 Malaysians in AI by 2029
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — MyDigital Corporation will collaborate with Oracle to train 300,000 Malaysians in artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance employability and support career growth by 2029. “I have launched the Oracle Skills Development Initiative (SDI), a collaboration between Oracle and MyDigital Corporation, at Oracle AI Experience Live 2026. He added that the training will encompass technologies such as Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), OCI Generative AI, OCI Data Science, Oracle APEX, OCI DevOps and OCI Security.

items.title: Govt plans mediation centre to help B40, M40 resolve disputes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Putrajaya is considering establishing a Malaysia Mediation Centre (MMC) to expand access to justice for the B40 and M40 income groups. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the cross-ministry initiative aims to support small contractors, freelancers, and service providers such as videographers and event planners who face payment disputes. "The government is considering the establishment of the Malaysia Mediation Centre under the Legal Aid Department, supported by a legal framework provided under the Legal Aid and Public Defence Act 2025.

items.title: Transit house caretaker and boat skipper among eight Indonesians under arrest for migrant smuggling in Pontian
items.summary: Pontian Police chief Supt Hadzrat Hussien Mion Hussain said the suspects, aged between 21 and 36, were picked up during the joint operation between the district police’s Criminal Investigation Division and the Kukup Region Two Marine Police. He said that during a Wednesday operation at 8 pm, five individuals were arrested, comprising a suspected caretaker of a transit house, a boat skipper, and three migrants. Hadzrat Hussien said the police also seized two boats and three mobile phones of various brands, with the total estimated at RM50,000.

items.title: JPJ eyes stricter DUI penalties, victim compensation after fatal crashes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has now begun a review and revision of several sections under the Road Transport Act (APJ) 1987 [Act 333] to allow drivers under the influence of alcohol or drugs to be subject to heavier penalties. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said JPJ, through its legal division, had begun a review and further study of two sections, namely Section 44 and Section 45 of APJ 1987. “The Minister of Transport has ordered the MOT and also the JPJ to review Section 44 and Section 45 under APJ.

items.title: Survival mode: Kelantan villagers forced to dig wells amid frequent water disruptions
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Since January, persistent water supply disruptions have forced villagers in Kampung Banggol Tok Mokyong, a community of about 2,000 people, to dig tube wells manually to access underground water. "We have been facing water problems for months. Ariffin spent about RM1,000 on pipes and basic equipment to build a simple well himself.

items.title: Wang Kelian meth seizure: Three to face High Court over 510kg haul, penalty includes death
items.summary: KANGAR, April 3 — Three individuals, including a married couple, were charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking 510 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine in Wang Kelian last month. The accused, Mohd Rizuan Ramli, 37, and the married couple Muhammad Amir Aswad Ismail, 27, and Nur Atiqa Anis Ramadha Abdullah, 24, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Nurul Natasha Rizal. According to the charge, the three individuals were jointly accused of trafficking 510,000 grammes of methamphetamine at the main road near the mini tar junction at a TNB substation in Wang Kelian, Jalan Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit near Padang Besar at about 9.30 am on March 24.

items.title: A-G explains murder charge for fatal drink-driving crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Attorney-General (A-G) Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar has defended the decision to charge a driver with murder in the fatal drink-driving crash in Klang, citing evidence the act was "so imminently dangerous" that it would, in all probability, cause death. He highlighted the accused's deliberate act of driving into the opposite lane at high speed, which created a highly perilous situation for the victim. "The act of the accused entering the opposing lane intentionally and at high speed resulted in a situation that was 'so imminently dangerous' to the deceased, as provided under Section 300(d) of the Penal Code," he said.

items.title: Eight arrested for using fake bank slips to purchase goods via WhatsApp
items.summary: TEMERLOH: Police have arrested eight men suspected of being part of a syndicate that used fake bank transfer slips to purchase goods via WhatsApp. The suspects were detained in a raid in Cheras, Selangor, following intelligence gathered by the Pahang Commercial Crime Investigation Department and the Temerloh police headquarters. Temerloh police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Nasyim Bahron, said the arrests followed a report by a 33-year-old saleswoman who claimed losses of RM7,575 after being deceived by the syndicate.

items.title: Police hunt man filmed in viral sexual harassment incident at Kajang MRT
items.summary: KAJANG, April 3 — Police are actively searching for a man captured in a viral video allegedly committing an act of sexual harassment at the Kajang MRT station. Kajang district police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof confirmed in a statement late Thursday that a 23-second video of the incident began circulating on the social media platform Threads at approximately 9.00am. Efforts to track down and arrest the suspect are actively ongoing,” Naazron stated.

items.title: Four-day remand for meth-positive trailer driver in JB-Segamat crash that killed van driver and married couple
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — The trailer driver involved in a fatal accident involving three vehicles at Kilometre 212, Jalan Johor Bahru-Seremban near Taman Golden Hill intersection, Gemas Baharu, yesterday evening was remanded for four days starting today until next Monday. Segamat district police chief, Supt. Mohd Jumazanzahir said in the incident yesterday evening, the victims who died were a married couple and the van driver.

items.title: Refiners expected to cut runs as hedgable economics drive decisions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Refiners are expected to reduce operating runs as decisions are driven by hedgable economics rather than directional market views, said independent research and energy intelligence company Rystad Energy. Its chief oil analyst, Paola Rodriguez-Masiu, said while nothing appears structurally broken for refiners on paper, conditions on the ground point to a strained Asian refining market scrambling for additional barrels. "Asian refiners, shut out of Middle Eastern supply, are bidding aggressively for every available Atlantic Basin barrel," she said.

items.title: Trump announces destruction of Iran's tallest bridge
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Thursday the tallest bridge in Iran had been destroyed, hours after threatening to bomb the country "back to the Stone Ages." Ghodratollah Seif, the deputy governor of Alborz province, where the B1 bridge is located, said the strike had killed eight people and wounded 95 others,according to state TV and Fars news agency. Trump said.

items.title: Will the Mideast conflict lead to a new international order?
items.summary: THE Iran war, with unclear and even changing objectives, takes the world into uncharted territory. With this latest war, the US has even dispensed with the pretence of obtaining the sanction of the United Nations. This is most regrettable since modern-day international rules have been largely the legacy of US leadership in the post-World War 2 world.

items.title: HLIB trims Malaysia's 2026 GDP to 4.5pct on global energy crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) trimmed its 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) to 4.5 per cent from 4.7 per cent following the global energy crisis resulting from the conflict in the Middle East. It noted that Malaysia's position as a net oil and gas exporter (2025: RM18 billion) is primarily driven by LNG (RM45 billion) and, to a smaller extent, refined petroleum products (RM3 billion). "Consequently, after factoring in the temporary energy supply disruption, we have trimmed our 2026 GDP growth forecast from 4.7 per cent year-on-year to 4.5 per cent," it said.

items.title: Fiscal support, stronger ringgit to cushion Middle East headwinds: Kenanga IB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Headwinds from the Middle East conflict are expected to be eased by fiscal support, including Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA), a stronger ringgit, and resilience in food and beverage spending, Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) said. In a research note, the investment bank said essential spending is taking priority and believes consumption is likely to remain skewed towards necessities in the near term, resulting in softer discretionary spending should cost pressures intensify. From a portfolio perspective, the investment bank said heavyweight names remain broadly neutral.

items.title: 5E Resources set for earnings growth on high barriers, ESG strength, says RHB Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Waste management company 5E Resources Holdings Bhd is expected to see strong earnings growth, according to RHB Research. RHB Research noted that this new facility will introduce advanced recovery technologies and expand 5E's licensed waste categories. 5E provides end-to-end services for industrial hazardous waste, including collection, recovery, and recycling.

items.title: Penang to decide on civil service WFH arrangements next week
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 3 — The Penang state government will discuss the implementation of work-from-home (WFH) arrangements for its civil servants next week, following a new federal directive aimed at reducing national fuel consumption. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow noted that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had met with all chief ministers and menteri besar yesterday to discuss the matter. The state executive council and the state secretary will meet next Wednesday to determine the scope and any additional guidelines needed, Chow said.

items.title: ‘When Malaysians create, Malaysia will grow’: Economy minister says local TikTok content creators driving jobs, digital economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Malaysian youths are redefining what work and enterprise can look like in the digital age through content creation, livestreaming, affiliate marketing and digital entrepreneurship on TikTok and TikTok Shop. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said young Malaysians are not just consuming digital content, but they are creating it, innovating through it, and building businesses around it. “This also includes opportunities linked to the digital economy, green economy, blue economy, circular economy and silver economy to diversify youth income sources,” he said.

items.title: Eskayvie targets regional growth as global wellness market expands
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The global wellness industry, including Malaysia, continues to experience strong post-pandemic expansion as consumers increasingly prioritise preventive healthcare, immunity support, and mental wellbeing. Driven by this structural shift in consumer behaviour, the health and wellness sector has evolved into a multi-trillion-ringgit global economy, with demand accelerating across supplements, functional foods, skincare, and lifestyle-based preventive care solutions. Riding this wave is Eskayvie, a homegrown halal-certified health and wellness brand, which is positioning itself for aggressive regional expansion to capture emerging opportunities across Southeast Asia.

items.title: Malaysia invites Turkmenistan to Maha 2026 as bilateral trade talks focus on food security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — Malaysia and Turkmenistan have agreed to explore more market opportunities for local agricultural products and boost bilateral trade for mutual benefit. The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (MAFS) said that the discussion took place during a courtesy call by Turkmenistan’s Ambassador to Malaysia, Muhammetnyyaz Mashalov, on Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu recently. “This inaugural meeting provided an opportunity for both parties to explore potential cooperation in the agriculture and food security sectors, as well as to strengthen existing trade relations,” the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Alcohol, drugs push road deaths to 31 last year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fatal road accidents involving drivers under the influence of alcohol and drugs have risen over the past two years. Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department statistics show 27 fatal cases in 2024, increasing to 31 last year. He said roadblocks and random screening tests are among the most effective ways to curb drunk driving.

items.title: Govt vows probe into alleged abuse at unregistered children's home
items.summary: BATU CAVES: The government has pledged a thorough investigation into alleged abuse at an unregistered children's home in Taman Selayang Permata, as more victims continue to come forward. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said enforcement action had been taken after authorities discovered the facility was operating illegally. She said the Gombak district social welfare office acted promptly following a report lodged on March 30.

items.title: Penang kicks off RM103m solar farm project that could power thousands of homes by 2027
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 3 — Penang has officially broken ground on a new RM103 million large-scale solar (LSS5) farm in Byram, Nibong Tebal, a project slated to generate 58,296 megawatt-hours (MWh) of clean energy annually starting from 2027. Chow said that the Byram solar farm complements other green initiatives in the state, including plans for floating solar installations at the Mengkuang and Teluk Bahang dams. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow is briefed on the LSS5 project in Byram, Nibong Tebal on April 3, 2026.

items.title: Carrying humanity's stories into the cosmos
items.summary: Rockets rise, trajectories are calculated, and humanity once again reaches outward. Long before spacecraft, the Moon lived in our stories. The Moon is not new to us.

items.title: Swiss man arrested at KLIA for smuggling 3.32kg of heroin
items.summary: fuadnizam@nst.com.my KUALA LUMPUR: A Swiss national's attempt to smuggle 3.32kg of heroin concealed inside a water hose reel was foiled after he was detained upon arrival at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA). KLIA Customs Department director Zulkifli Muhammad said the drugs were detected with the assistance of personnel from the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA). "KLIA MCBA personnel detained a Swiss national at the arrival hall of Terminal 2.

items.title: Boh joins Warisan Kuala Lumpur Initiative at Sultan Abdul Samad Building
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Boh Plantations has been invited to participate in Khazanah Nasional Bhd's Dana Warisan Kuala Lumpur (Warisan KL) restoration initiative at Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad, one of Malaysia's most significant, historic and architecturally rich landmarks in the city centre. Boh chairman Caroline Russell said the company's participation in Warisan KL at Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad is especially meaningful, as it allows Boh to celebrate both its own legacy and Malaysia's broader national story, shaped by its people, places, and shared history. Boh's presence at Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad reflects a shared history dating back to the formative years of Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Pro-Palestinian flotilla's new Gaza mission to start in Spain on April 12
items.summary: BARCELONA: A flotilla of pro-Palestinian activists who attempted to reach Gaza last year said on Thursday they would launch a new mission to the devastated territory from Barcelona on April 12. The Global Sumud Flotilla's first weeks-long journey across the Mediterranean Sea to Gaza, blockaded by Israel during the war against Palestinian group Hamas, drew worldwide attention. Israel's interception of their boats and arrests of the activists as they approached Gaza, which suffered severe shortages of food, water, medicine and fuel, sparked international condemnation.

items.title: NGOs lack confidence in AG-PP appointment process, says Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Non-governmental organisations have expressed a lack of confidence in the proposed appointment process under Putrajaya's bill to separate the roles of the Attorney-General and Public Prosecutor. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the government will review the feedback and refine the bill to ensure the proposed constitutional amendment secures a two-thirds majority in Parliament. Azalina said cessation of employment was also among the issues raised.

items.title: Paris police ban French Muslim gathering
items.summary: PARIS: The Paris police on Thursday banned an annual gathering of French Muslims north of the capital, citing a "major terrorist risk" that it might be targeted. The order banning it issued by the Paris police said there was a risk that "small far-right groups" might try to disrupt the event. But the police order said the event was "taking place in a particularly tense international and national context."

items.title: Kedah siblings forced out of school due to poverty
items.summary: KUALA NERANG: Poverty has kept two siblings in Naka out of school, leaving them without formal education. Their mother, Siti Nur Jamilah Jamaludin, 39, said their situation worsened after her mother's death and her husband left several years ago. The single mother said she feels guilty seeing her children miss out on education and often ask when they can attend school like their friends.

items.title: Penang launches solar project to cut carbon footprint, boost employment
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Penang today launched the groundbreaking of the Large-Scale Solar 5 (LSS5) project in Byram, a major initiative to reduce the state's carbon footprint while creating skilled employment opportunities. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the shift to solar energy was crucial in tackling climate change. Chow said the ability to deliver a stable and green energy supply would continue to attract high-technology investments into the state.

items.title: Swiss national faces court after Customs find 3.3kg of heroin hidden in luggage at KLIA2
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — An attempt to smuggle 3.3 kilogrammes (kg) of heroin worth nearly RM266,000 was foiled at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2 last February. KLIA Customs director Zulkifli Muhammad said the suspect, who was attempting to enter Malaysia from a Southeast Asian country, was stopped after a scan of a water hose reel box he was carrying revealed suspicious images. “A thorough inspection by the Customs enforcement team uncovered black packages concealed inside the water hose reel, believed to contain heroin,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Matrade secures over RM200mil in trade deals at China food and drinks fair
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade), in collaboration with Messe Worldwide Sdn Bhd, has secured more than RM200 million in trade value at the recently concluded China Food and Drinks Fair (CFDF) in Sichuan, China. CFDF is considered to be China's longest-running and most influential trade exhibition, serving as a powerful gateway for global food and beverage brands to tap into the world's largest consumer market. China and Malaysia's bilateral trade in 2025 hit RM541.54 billion, with the Sichuan province emerging as one of the country's top 10 provincial trading partners within China, accounting for RM18.85 billion of the total trade volume.

items.title: BDR doesn’t mean ‘Baring, duduk, rehat’: Chief Secretary warns civil servants as remote work policy starts April 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The upcoming work-from-home (WFH) policy is not a licence for civil servants to neglect their duties, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar warned today. Set to begin on April 15, he said the WFH initiative demands the same discipline and productivity as working from the office and must be carried out with strict monitoring by department heads, Sinar Harian reported. “Output and productivity must remain the same, as officers implementing WFH need to have clear KPIs (key performance indicators) that are set in advance.” Shamsul Azri explained that the policy is part of a civil service reform that prioritises work results over mere physical attendance.

items.title: MARKET PULSE AM APRIL 3, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia opened higher today, tracking a mixed performance on Wall Street after US President Donald Trump's latest televised address offered no clear resolution to the Middle East conflict. While valuations appear increasingly attractive at current levels, traders are largely staying on the sidelines as they await greater clarity. In the crypto market, Bitcoin fell to RM269,686 amid ongoing tensions.

items.title: Geopolitical tensions threaten FMCG packaging supply, says CIMB Securities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A prolonged disruption stemming from geopolitical tensions could tighten packaging availability across the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector, beyond just Farm Fresh Bhd, according to CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd. In a note, CIMB Securities said Farm Fresh has experienced disruptions to its high-density polyethylene (HDPE) resin supply chain over the past 10 to 14 days, following the US-Iran conflict. Beyond these immediate pressures, Farm Fresh has flagged potential second-order risks if the conflict persists.

items.title: Penang cops crack gold robbery gang, recover 4.3kg of stolen gold worth RM3.6m, arrest seven Malaysians and two Indonesians
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 3 — Penang police have crippled an armed robbery gang after arresting nine individuals linked to the robbery of gold worth RM3.6 million in several raids across the state under Op Jingga D Garden. State police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the operations, conducted from March 26 until yesterday, involved a team of officers and personnel from the Criminal Investigation Department of the Penang contingent police headquarters (IPK), together with the Criminal Investigation Division of the Seberang Perai Utara district police headquarters. He said checks found that three of the suspects have serious past criminal records, including one with 17 records related to criminal offences and one drug case.

items.title: DoE director-general pleads not guilty to RM100k bribe [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Environment director-general has pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to accepting a RM100,000 bribe linked to an environmental approval process in Penang. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib proposed bail at RM100,000, citing the accused's position, the amount involved, and the severity of the punishment if convicted. Lawyer Mohd Firdaus Ahmad, representing the accused, requested a lower bail of RM20,000 to RM30,000, arguing the prosecution's proposal was excessive.

items.title: MCMC cracks down on 17 people over misleading posts on fuel and electricity prices
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 3 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is investigating 17 individuals for allegedly spreading false information on fuel price hikes and new electricity tariffs that have gone viral on social media platforms. It said that, to date, statements have been recorded from eight individuals, including in five cases involving false posts related to fuel price hikes. “Of the five cases, statements from three suspects were recorded earlier, while the remaining two were recorded yesterday.

items.title: DoE chief charged over RM100,000 bribe in Penang road project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The director-general of the Environment Department, Datuk Wan Abdul Latiff Wan Jaffar, was charged at the Sessions Court here today with accepting a RM100,000 bribe in connection with an appeal to bypass an environmental impact assessment (EIA) for a major road project in Penang. Wan Abdul Latiff, 58, entered a plea of not guilty after the charge was read out to him before Judge Suzana Hussin, Free Malaysia Today reported. According to the charge sheet, he is accused of receiving the bribe from Datuk Khor Chong Hai, a director of Sunrich Conquest Sdn Bhd, through the company’s project manager, Lee Ham Kong.

items.title: JB-Seremban road crash kills three in van; trailer driver tests positive for meth
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — Three individuals were killed while two others sustained minor injuries in a road accident involving three vehicles at KM212 of the Johor Bahru-Seremban road yesterday. Johor police chief Datuk CP Ab Rahaman Arsad said the incident, which occurred at around 3.45 pm, is believed to have happened when a trailer driven by a local man crashed into the rear of a van carrying the victims. He added that three victims who were in the van were pronounced dead at the scene due to severe injuries.

items.title: Malaysian doctors, nurses head to Mecca for haj duty next week as West Asia armed conflict rages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A total of 225 Malaysian medical personnel will depart for the Holy Land next week to serve during the 1447H/2026 haj season. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said that despite ongoing geopolitical challenges in West Asia, haj operations remain well-managed through strong diplomacy by the Madani government under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and cooperation with the Saudi Arabian government. “The absence of a Tabung Haji (TH) treatment centre means pilgrims will be referred directly to Saudi government hospitals, requiring personnel to demonstrate proactiveness, patience and diplomacy,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: US-Iran war crisis shows Malaysia not built to survive an oil shock, think tank warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The global oil shock triggered by the US-Israel war against Iran has exposed a fundamental weakness in Malaysia’s economic structure: its overwhelming dependence on cars and subsidised fuel, according to environmental think tank RimbaWatch. The think tank calculates that if just half of the Klang Valley’s workforce worked from home, the government could save RM169 million in fuel subsidies every month, or over RM2 billion annually. “Klang Valley commuters waste an estimated 580 hours in traffic every year, costing them an estimated RM9,000 annually in vehicle maintenance and fuel,” the group said.

items.title: Explosion sparks fire at Chinese prayer shop in Kuala Krai; owners escape with burns (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA KRAI, April 3 — A couple suffered burns after their shop selling Chinese religious items at Central Park here caught fire Wednesday night. Kuala Krai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Nik Ahmad Afsham Nik Pa said the department received an emergency call at about 8.30 pm before dispatching a firefighting team. He said a total of 21 personnel from BBP Kuala Krai, BBP Sungai Durian and the Guchil Volunteer Fire Team were involved in the operation.

items.title: Malaysia and China ink 26 deals worth RM289m in trade, AI and agriculture at Belt-and-Road dialogue in Ningxia
items.summary: YINCHUAN (China), April 3 — A total of 26 cooperation projects worth 495 million yuan (about US$72 million; RM289.99 million) were signed during the Sixth Belt and Road China-Malaysia Business Dialogue, which opened on Thursday in Yinchuan, capital of northwest China’s Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region, reported Xinhua. The deals, covering areas such as economic and trade investment and modern agriculture, represent a significant expansion of bilateral economic ties. China has been Malaysia’s largest trading partner for several consecutive years.

items.title: Immigration Dept nab three Chinese nationals in KL suspected of using forged MM2H stickers to aid US visa applications
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — The Immigration Department (JIM) has detained three Chinese nationals for allegedly using fake social visit passes as supporting documents in their applications for visas to the United States. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that the three, a man and two women aged between 25 and 54, were arrested around the federal capital at 11.25 am yesterday. He said the department also seized three passports bearing Social Visit Pass (Dependent) stickers from the Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) programme, which were suspected to be fake.

items.title: Soaring diesel prices ground 4x4 aid missions, jeopardising lifeline for Malaysia’s remote Orang Asli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A sharp spike in diesel prices is forcing volunteer 4x4 teams, who provide a crucial lifeline to remote indigenous communities, to halt their aid missions, leaving vulnerable villages without essential supplies. Off-roading enthusiast Frankie Chan, who leads a team delivering food and medicine to Orang Asli villages in the jungle, said the financial burden has become unsustainable. Filling up feels like a heartache,” Chan said, noting that the cost to fill his truck's 80-litre tank has jumped from approximately RM240 to RM480.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest: Psychiatrist says dorm confrontation was key trigger, not sole cause
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 3 — A consultant forensic psychiatrist told the Coroner’s Court here today that a confrontation between Zara Qairina Mahathir and her senior in a dormitory acted as a triggering and precipitating factor the night before the teenager was found unconscious at the school hostel on July 16. Dr Chua Sze Hung, 43, a forensic psychiatrist at Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang in Kota Kinabalu, said the Form One student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Agama Tun Datu Mustapha, Papar, appeared to have been deprived of her usual coping mechanisms on the night of the incident. “On the night of the incident, after the confrontation (in the room dormitory), I believed that she (Zara Qairina) was devoid of her usual coping mechanisms,” he told the inquest’s conducting officer, deputy public prosecutor Mohd Fairuz Johari, before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan.

items.title: How Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 evolved from 11MP plans to a hub for high-tech industries
items.summary: Malaysia Vision Valley (MVV), first launched under the 11th Malaysia Plan in 2015, aims to expand development beyond the Klang Valley and drive regional economic growth. The project was rebranded as Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0) in 2018, now spanning 153,411 hectares in Negeri Sembilan and targeting RM294 billion in investments over 30 years. KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Malaysia Vision Valley (MVV) was first introduced in 2015 as part of the government’s broader strategy to expand development beyond the Klang Valley and drive regional economic growth.

items.title: Students, teachers flee as fire engulfs dormitory at JB tahfiz school, no casualties
items.summary: Principal Mohamad Halimi Kolan said he heard cracking sounds from the upper floor housing the dormitory and hall before asking a teacher to check. “I heard cracking sounds from the upper floor which houses the dormitory and hall. Meanwhile, the Johor Fire and Rescue Department’s Operations Centre said it received an emergency call at 4.35 pm before dispatching a fire engine from the Larkin Fire and Rescue Station to the location.

items.title: Police investigate death of 13-year-old girl in Sungai Buloh, suspect abuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A 13-year-old girl was found dead at a residence in Sungai Buloh near here Wednesday. Sungai Buloh district police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said police received information on the victim being found unconscious at 6.27 pm. He said a medical officer from Sungai Buloh Hospital who arrived at the scene confirmed that the victim had died.

items.title: Minister vows action after army corporal’s assault leaves ex-commando’s son with brain damage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — A brutal assault by an army corporal that left a 24-year-old soldier with brain damage has drawn a vow of firm action from Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. The victim, 24-year-old Muhammad Amirul Raziq, remains in critical condition after the alleged assault of seven recruits at a Royal Malay Regiment camp. The victim’s father, Rosafindi Tambi Chik, a former army commando, condemned the act, stating that military training never taught personnel to assault fellow soldiers.
